item.text: The gallery context tests are patched to exercise the new peer-clear behavior directly, including the null-default case. Next I’m wiring the timeline hook so plain timeline selection clears gallery state and gallery-triggered peer clears only clear timeline state.

Ran the full test file with `npx vitest run src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx`; it passed with 8/8 tests, including the 'no peer clear registered' case confirming non-toggle selection does not throw when the peer ref remains null.","files_changed":["src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx"],"commands_run":["sed -n '1,260p' src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx","git status --short src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts","sed -n '1,280p' src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx","npx vitest run src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx","nl -ba src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx | sed -n '1,260p'"]},{"task_id":"T3","status":"done","executor_notes":"Wired `useTimelineState` to consume `clearGallerySelection` and `registerPeerClear` from `useGallerySelection` (line 66). Verified plain timeline selection paths clear gallery first in `selectClip` (lines 68-88), `selectClips` (lines 90-100), `clearSelection` (lines 114-118), and the `selectedClipId` sync layout effect (lines 155-162), with `clearGallerySelection` added to each dependency array. Added `clearTimelineOnly` plus peer registration effect at lines 120-130; this callback only calls `clearSelectionState()` and `dataHook.setSelectedClipId(null)`, so gallery-originated peer clears do not recurse back into gallery clearing. Full video-editor regression execution is deferred to T4.","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts"],"commands_run":["sed -n '1,260p' src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts","git status --short src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts","sed -n '1,220p' src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts","nl -ba src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts | sed -n '1,190p'"]}],"sense_check_acknowledgments":[{"sense_check_id":"SC2","executor_note":"Yes. The five peer-clear cases were added at `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx:167-234`, and the full test file passed via `npx vitest run src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx` with 8/8 tests green. The 'allows non-toggle selection when no peer clear is registered' case confirms no error when `peerClearRef` is still null."},{"sense_check_id":"SC3","executor_note":"Yes. Timeline-originated plain selection paths call `clearGallerySelection()` directly in `useTimelineState` (`selectClip`, `selectClips`, `clearSelection`, and the `selectedClipId` sync effect), and `clearGallerySelection` remains a direct gallery state clear rather than a selection action. Gallery-originated peer clear now resolves to `clearTimelineOnly()` in `useTimelineState:120-130`, which only runs `clearSelectionState()` and `dataHook.setSelectedClipId(null)` and never calls `clearGallerySelection()`. Both directions terminate without a loop."}]}

item.text: I’ve got a path to verify the real cross-source wiring without touching product code: a temporary Vitest harness that uses the real `GallerySelectionProvider` and `useTimelineState`, with the non-selection editor dependencies mocked down to no-ops. I’m adding that file now, then I’ll run it and delete it.

Added a temporary Vitest harness at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/__tmp_unifiedSelectionVerification.test.tsx`, ran it with `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/hooks/__tmp_unifiedSelectionVerification.test.tsx`, and confirmed both directions of the unified selection behavior: plain gallery selection clears timeline while gallery toggle preserves it, and plain timeline selection clears gallery while timeline toggle preserves it. The temporary file was then deleted, so there are no persistent file changes from T4.
